Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. The Collective Ambition Behind Odysseus, a Game-Changing Sci-Fi Larp 81 upvotes, 3 comments
  2. CasNum 321 upvotes, 38 comments
  3. Phi-4-reasoning-vision and the lessons of training a multimodal reasoning model 69 upvotes, 5 comments
  4. LibreOffice: Request to the European Commission to adhere to its own guidances 180 upvotes, 28 comments
  5. If It Quacks Like a Package Manager 35 upvotes, 3 comments
  6. FrameBook 175 upvotes, 33 comments
  7. Sem – Semantic version control. Entity-level diffs on top of Git 69 upvotes, 11 comments
  8. Some Words on WigglyPaint 88 upvotes, 16 comments
  9. Rijksmuseum researchers discover new painting by Rembrandt van Rijn 118 upvotes, 26 comments
  10. What if the Apple ][ had run on Field-Sequential? 44 upvotes, 7 comments
  11. LibreOffice Writer now supports Markdown 69 upvotes, 14 comments
  12. Show HN: Eyot, A programming language where the GPU is just another thread 55 upvotes, 11 comments
  13. How to run Qwen 3.5 locally 382 upvotes, 118 comments
  14. I ported Linux to the PS5 and turned it into a Steam Machine 240 upvotes, 89 comments
  15. Based on its own charter, OpenAI should surrender the race 211 upvotes, 78 comments
  16. SWE-CI: Evaluating Agent Capabilities in Maintaining Codebases via CI 100 upvotes, 35 comments
  17. Notes on Writing WASM 150 upvotes, 60 comments
  18. Cloud VM benchmarks 2026 307 upvotes, 136 comments
  19. CLI RSS/Atom feed reader inspired by Taskwarrior, synced using Git 43 upvotes, 13 comments
  20. The Lobster Programming Language 104 upvotes, 42 comments
  21. Warn about PyPy being unmaintained 281 upvotes, 149 comments
  22. Apple's 512GB Mac Studio vanishes, a quiet acknowledgment of the RAM shortage 304 upvotes, 179 comments
  23. Log messages are mostly for the people operating your software 38 upvotes, 15 comments
  24. Beagle, a source code management system that stores AST trees 54 upvotes, 26 comments
  25. Accessing inactive union members through char 12 upvotes, 3 comments
  26. Claude Struggles to Cope with ChatGPT Exodus 49 upvotes, 30 comments
  27. Show HN: Skir – like Protocol Buffer but better 6 upvotes, 1 comments
  28. My Homelab Setup 39 upvotes, 26 comments
  29. Why can't you tune your guitar? (2019) 97 upvotes, 80 comments
  30. "I Can't Do That, Dave" – No Agent Yet 3 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 2:30am, 9 March 2026. Source here.
